title: Motor Yacht Rental in Puerto Banus: Costs and Tips for 2026
html:
Puerto Banus, the glamorous marina on Spain's Costa del Sol, offers exceptional motor yacht charter options. A half-day rental for eight guests typically starts at 2,500 EUR, with prices rising for larger vessels and longer durations. You can compare verified boat listings on BoatHire24 to find your ideal vessel.

How much does it cost to rent a motor yacht in Puerto Banus?

Rental prices start from around 1,200 EUR for a small motorboat for half a day and exceed 10,000 EUR for a fully crewed luxury yacht for a full day. The exact cost depends on the boat size, age, and season. A typical Sunseeker 68 Sport costs about 3,500 EUR for a half-day charter including fuel and crew. You can compare verified boat listings for your chosen dates.

  • Shared tour per person: from 150 EUR for a 3-hour trip
  • Small motorboat (half-day, 6 guests): from 1,200 EUR
  • Catamaran full-day (12 guests): from 2,500 EUR
  • Crewed motor yacht full-day (10 guests): from 5,000 EUR

High season runs from June to September, when demand peaks and prices may be 20-30% higher. The prevailing Poniente wind can create choppy conditions in the afternoon, so morning departures are often recommended.

Do you need a licence to rent a motor yacht in Puerto Banus?

For motor yachts under 15 metres (50 feet) with 150 horsepower, no licence is required; larger vessels typically require a valid ICC or equivalent. The operator will confirm exact requirements based on the boat's specifications. Most rental companies include a skipper for larger yachts, which bypasses any licence concerns.

Book a morning departure to enjoy calmer seas before the Poniente wind picks up; the afternoon breeze can make returning to the marina more challenging.

Where to set off in Puerto Banus

Most motor yacht charters depart from Marina Puerto Banus itself, the heart of the destination. Nearby, the port of Marbella offers alternative departure points, and Gibraltar is approximately 40 nautical miles south-west. For day trips, head to the Bay of Gibraltar or anchor off Estepona for a quieter swim stop. The island of Alboran is a longer passage, about 30 nautical miles south, but its marine reserve is a unique destination for serious explorers.

Which boat to rent

  1. Sunseeker 68 Sport, ideal for a stylish day trip with up to 10 guests, from 3,500 EUR half-day
  2. Azimut 50 Fly, perfect for cruising the coast with 8 guests, from 2,800 EUR half-day
  3. Axopar 37, a sporty open boat for up to 6 guests, from 1,200 EUR half-day
  4. Lagoon 630 MY, a spacious motor yacht for 12 guests, from 5,000 EUR full-day

For a closer look at a popular model, see this motor yacht listing on BoatHire24, which includes full specifications and booking details.

At a glance

  • Price band: 1,200, 10,000+ EUR
  • Peak months: June to September
  • Typical trip length: 4-8 hours
  • Capacity: 6 to 12 guests
  • Currency: EUR (Euro)

Questions about boat rental in Puerto Banus

Can I rent a boat without a skipper?

Yes, for smaller motorboats under 15 metres you may go bareboat if you hold a valid licence, but most guests prefer a skipper for larger yachts.

What is the best time of year to charter?

June through September offers the warmest weather and calmest seas, though July and August are the busiest.

Are fuel and crew included in the price?

Most inclusive charters cover fuel, crew, and insurance, but always check the listing details to confirm.

How far ahead should I book?

Book at least two weeks in advance during peak season to secure your preferred vessel.

Whether you are planning a luxury day out or a coastal exploration, renting a motor yacht in Puerto Banus is straightforward through BoatHire24. Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Puerto Banus and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
